name: Uganda

colors.symbolism: African brotherhood — the colour shared by all peoples through the blood in their veins
the disc behind the central crane
the body and wings of the grey crowned crane

description: Six equal horizontal stripes in repeating order — black, yellow, red, black, yellow, red — with a centred white disc bearing a grey crowned crane facing the hoist, one leg raised.
history: Adopted on 9 October 1962, the day Uganda gained independence from the United Kingdom. The colours and tricolour pattern are taken from the flag of the Uganda People's Congress, which won the pre-independence elections; the crested crane on the central white disc was already the colonial badge of the Uganda Protectorate and was retained as a national symbol.

features.symbolism: the colours of the Uganda People's Congress, the party that led the country to independence: black for the African people, yellow for the African sun, and red for African brotherhood
Uganda's national bird; depicted with one leg raised to symbolise the country moving forward; the bird had been used as the badge of Ugandan soldiers under British rule
